mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling corporate revenues and recession risk.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and sustaining positive equity market performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or show negative predictive power over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses equity valuations during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index signals disinflationary pressure that reduces near-term rate hike expectations, prompting immediate short-term equity rallies as discount rates compress.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL declines, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical fluctuation, forecasting prolonged earnings declines and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or shows positive predictive power during recovery phases over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating consistent positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp deceleration in industrial production growth during periods of restrictive monetary policy signals demand destruction from high borrowing costs, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors anticipate recession.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration coincides with positive 5-day returns in > 55% of restrictive policy periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.
